latest.title: Further extension of PUSU deadline
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1263D
Capricorn Energy PLC
06 May 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, IN, INTO, OR FROM ANY JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF THE RELEVANT LAWS OF THAT JURISDICTION
THIS IS AN ANNOUNCEMENT FALLING UNDER RULE 2.4 OF THE CITY CODE ON TAKEOVERS AND MERGERS (THE "CODE") AND DOES NOT CONSTITUTE AN ANNOUNCEMENT OF A FIRM INTENTION TO MAKE AN OFFER UNDER RULE 2.7 OF THE CODE. THERE CAN BE NO CERTAINTY THAT ANY FIRM OFFER WILL BE MADE NOR AS TO THE TERMS ON WHICH ANY FIRM OFFER MIGHT BE MADE
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
6 May 2026
Capricorn Energy PLC ("Capricorn Energy" or the "Company")
Further extension of PUSU deadline
On 11 March 2026, the Board of Directors of Capricorn Energy announced that it had received multiple unsolicited non-binding proposals from Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC ("Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah"), a member of the Cafani Group, regarding a possible all cash offer to acquire the entire issued and to be issued share capital of the Company (the "Possible Offer").
On 8 April 2026, the Company announced an extension of the deadline by which Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah was required either to announce a firm intention to make an offer for Capricorn Energy in accordance with Rule 2.7 of the Code or to announce that it does not intend to make an offer for Capricorn Energy, to 5.00 p.m. (London time) on 6 May 2026 (the "PUSU Deadline") in order to allow further time for Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah to progress its funding arrangements.
Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah continues to report progress regarding its funding arrangements and, accordingly, the Company has requested, and the Panel on Takeovers and Mergers (the "Panel") has consented to, a further extension of the PUSU Deadline in accordance with Rule 2.6(c) of the Code.
Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah is now required, by not later than 5.00 pm (London time) on 3 June 2026 (the "Revised PUSU Deadline"), either to announce a firm intention to make an offer for the Company in accordance with Rule 2.7 of the Code or announce that it does not intend to make an offer for the Company, in which case the announcement will be treated as a statement to which Rule 2.8 of the Code applies. This Revised PUSU Deadline may be further extended by the Company with the consent of the Panel in accordance with Rule 2.6(c) of the Code.
There can be no certainty that any firm offer will be made, nor as to the terms of any such firm offer.
A further announcement will be made as appropriate. In the meantime, shareholders are advised to take no action.
The Company remains in an "Offer Period" as defined in the Code. Accordingly, the dealing disclosure requirements listed below will continue to apply.
Contact Information:
Capricorn Energy plc
Randy Neely, Chief Executive Officer
+44 131 475 3000
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Financial Adviser and Corporate Broker)
Henry Fitzgerald-O'Connor, George Grainger
+44 20 7523 8000
Inside Information
The information contained within this announcement is deemed by Capricorn Energy to constitute inside information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) no. 596/2014 (as it forms part of domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018). On the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
The person responsible for the release of this announcement on behalf of Capricorn Energy is Paul Ervine, Company Secretary.
Disclaimer
Canaccord Genuity Limited ("Canaccord Genuity"), which is authorised and regulated in the United Kingdom by the Financial Conduct Authority, is acting exclusively for Capricorn Energy and no-one else in connection with the matters described in this announcement and will not be responsible to anyone other than Capricorn Energy for providing the protections afforded to clients of Canaccord Genuity nor for providing advice in relation to the subject matter of this announcement. Neither Canaccord Genuity nor any of its affiliates owes or accepts any duty, liability or responsibility whatsoever (whether direct or indirect, whether in contract, in tort, under statute or otherwise) to any person who is not a client of Canaccord Genuity in connection with this announcement, any statement contained herein or otherwise.
Dealing Disclosure Requirements of the Code
Under Rule 8.3(a) of the Code, any person who is interested in 1% or more of any class of relevant securities of an offeree company or of any securities exchange offeror (being any offeror other than an offeror in respect of which it has been announced that its offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash) must make an Opening Position Disclosure following the commencement of the offer period and, if later, following the announcement in which any securities exchange offeror is first identified. An Opening Position Disclosure must contain details

company_30s.what_they_do: Capricorn Energy plc is a small gas producer that owns and operates a modest North Sea gas field. The company focuses on extracting and selling natural gas to local and regional markets, with a secondary aim of developing the asset further for long‑term supply.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The North Sea remains a strategic energy source for the UK, and any new or re‑energised field can help fill supply gaps as older wells age. Capricorn’s asset, while small, sits in a region with growing demand for cleaner gas, giving it a niche advantage over larger, more diversified players.
company_30s.current_state: The company is currently in a distressed phase, having released a series of regulatory filings and annual reports that highlight cash burn and limited revenue. It appears to be seeking ways to stabilise—either through a partnership, a sale of the gas asset, or a fresh funding round. The share price is far below its historical peak, reflecting a sharp sell‑off.

rns_narrative: Capricorn has been busy filing the standard UK disclosure documents (Form 8.3, Form 8 for Deltic Energy, a holdings report, and an annual financial statement). These filings show the company is clarifying its ownership structure, confirming regulatory compliance, and providing a transparent view of its financial health.

A key theme is asset ownership and governance. The company is making sure its North Sea gas field is correctly reported and that any potential sale or partnership is disclosed early, which could reduce uncertainty for investors.

Another theme is financial stability. The annual report indicates ongoing cash burn, but the company is also outlining potential avenues for raising capital, either through equity or debt, to keep the field operational.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautious optimism: investors are watching for a deal or funding but remain wary of the company’s financial fragility.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the untapped value of the North Sea asset and suggest that a strategic partner or a new funding round could trigger a sharp rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter focuses on the company’s cash burn, the lack of recent revenue, and the risk that the gas field may never be monetised fully, leading to further dilution or even bankruptcy.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from pure dread to a measured hope as more disclosures hint at possible deals. The conversation is still quiet, but the few bullish posts hint that a catalyst might be on the horizon.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent spikes, meaning the market has largely ignored the stock—ideal for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: A formal bid or partnership proposal for the North Sea field—this could be announced in the next RNS or a press release.
Completion of any pending regulatory approvals that would allow the company to operate the gas field or to sell it.
A funding round (equity or debt) that injects cash into the balance sheet, signalling confidence from investors.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in share price accompanied by volume that is at least twice the average daily volume, and a new RNS that announces a deal or funding. A clear break above the last low and a sustained move above that level would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of capitulation—price is low, volume is thin, and sentiment is muted. A bounce will likely require a catalyst that restores confidence and a spike in buying activity. The APEX score suggests the price is compressed, so a breakout could be imminent once the catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company secures a partnership or a funding round, the gas asset’s value could be realised, lifting the price to roughly halfway between its current level and the 52‑week high. That would double the investment. Historically, deep crash setups (70‑95% down) have delivered 2‑3x returns when the catalyst arrives.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company could fail to secure financing, the asset might be deemed non‑viable, or the partnership could fall through, pushing the price further down. In that worst‑case scenario, the downside could reach the lower 20‑30p range, representing a near‑total loss of the current stake.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 2‑3x the downside. If you lose 70% of the stake, you could gain 140‑210% if the bounce hits the mid‑peak. The asymmetry makes this an attractive contrarian bet.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the recent low with volume 2× the average. Size the position to 5‑10% of the portfolio, and set a stop 10% below the breakout level to protect against a false move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume spike and a news announcement of a deal or funding. Trigger a trade only after a clear, sustained move above the breakout level.
playbook.if_passing: Only consider abandoning the position if the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or the gas asset is confirmed to be non‑viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Capricorn Energy is a deeply crashed gas producer that could bounce dramatically once a partnership or funding deal materialises, offering a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian opportunity.
